evidence: The inherited W4d ledger closed with dual NO_BLOCKER after round 10 on 5abdad5a and bound the then-current 93-row manifest; W4f subsequently opened a new adjudication over the path-type freeze package.

W4f round 1 on 9cc3a788 returned statistics BLOCKED on S1 rule/code numerical mismatch, S2 exclusion taxonomy, and S3 protected-v2 byte drift. Round 2 remediation 81c684a3 closed S2 and S3 but retained the S1 exact-boundary fixture residual. Round 3 d1dc2a30 replaced the masked 45-degree case with bit-exact and adjacent-side fixtures and made every reviewed boundary assertion strict.

Round-3 confirmation returned statistics NO_BLOCKER and execution first-look NO_BLOCKER on the exact 101-row review manifest at SHA-256 420699d21efa7123dbccf9c75d7401c83c1ef50368f171d350c61aa5264dff6b, with zero unresolved blockers or majors.
findings.summary: The W4f S1-S3 review ledger closes with dual NO_BLOCKER on the bit-exact path-type freeze package.

evidence: The v1p7 path-type addendum and stdlib generator now share the same finite-binary64 comparison contract: tstar 1e-12 s, OLS denominator 1e-18 s^2, direction speed 1e-9 m/s, and direct class-boundary comparisons without epsilon.

The 45-degree fixture evaluates exactly to 45.0 and is bracketed by 44.999999999999986 and 45.00000000000001 outcomes; 135 degrees, lateral 4 m and 5 m, and longitudinal -8 m are also asserted by exact equality.

The rating-blind 254-row table remains CP=115, HO=90, MP=48, F=1; the other geometry-available 222 scenes terminate at F_MISSING_WOD_PATH_TYPE while three structural no-geometry scenes terminate at K before lookup.
findings.summary: The frozen classification rule, boundary semantics, and K/F attrition are single-valued and internally consistent.

evidence: RQ014_envelope_builder_contract_v2.json is restored to protected SHA-256 407d63209764896a673aa94811f9dd8b60a57a047d17e8cee0a3465c55b8c8a4; new mapping detail is confined to the versioned addendum and v1p6 registry.
The active 12-value science binding checksum-binds the definition, implementation and mapping table while preserving the previously reviewed M3-fixed 320-cell and 960-row lane-v3 authority.
No rating value was accessed and no HPC job was submitted during any statistics review in the W4f ledger.
findings.summary: Protected history, rating blindness, and the preflight-only authority boundary remain intact.

evidence: After the W4f Formal G1 and final bundle were published, operator preflight job 1922605 failed before any cell, ledger or science work because its G2 manifest named the blind-anchor receipt in the managed checkout while the closed job snapshot allowed a different root set.

The root-cause ruling selected defect class (b): launcher and runtime enforced incompatible path policies, so neither the checkout receipt nor an installed copy could satisfy both phases at commit 218d8bd2; the other three G2 input roles remained within their allowed roots.

Fix 7ed30065 binds the rating-free blind-anchor role to inputs/RQ014/blind_anchor/v1/RQ014_blind_anchor_receipt_v1p5.json at exactly 1,752 bytes and SHA-256 80e393f73e353e19da4d280ca946a6e7dcee3197824f723155944829f295496a through one validator shared by launcher and job execution.

The terminal W4h statistics and execution-governance reviews returned dual NO_BLOCKER on the exact 101-row manifest SHA-256 4fb4af0cfc452352e08a62ee5c8f14517e077edcaefb27310442967b2591bc68, with zero unresolved blockers or majors and no rating access during review.
findings.summary: The W4h cross-phase blind-anchor defect is closed without changing the frozen science or rating-blind boundary.

evidence: W5a round 1 reviewed commit 06ffd12d083b7fe0672f02509328de11d87e11d1 and its deterministic 102-row candidate manifest. Statistics returned BLOCKED only on prose: the D2 decision's blanket bundle-regeneration denial conflicted with the required final checksum bundle, the run-spec README described the newly allowlisted pilot as centrally unauthorized, and START_HERE retained the stale pending-preflight-acceptance/D2 stop wording; execution governance returned NO_BLOCKER.

The option-(c) remedy amended W5a to 2c8706e8b527d50474abd7b37564b1634398a3b9 without changing authorization semantics: scientific/input-bundle regeneration remains forbidden, the section 8.1 final checksum bundle is expressly permitted but non-executing, and README plus START_HERE now enumerate the implementation, fresh-G1/bundle, validate-only and explicit-PI-confirmation gates.

Round 2 returned statistics NO_BLOCKER and execution-governance NO_BLOCKER on the exact 102-row review manifest SHA-256 4f24e243575995015aa294abf2dfc2288928ad6066456e9ff9ec87307d37cf07, with zero unresolved blockers or majors; central allowlisting remains implementation-closure-only and does not authorize validate-only, submission or execution.
findings.summary: The W5a round-1 prose blocker is remedied and round 2 closes with dual NO_BLOCKER without broadening execution authority.

evidence: W5b round 1 reviewed commit 4c7bd1be099f233d61c683127ef3e40b24bd95f2 and returned three statistics blockers: S1 required fail-closed validation of the ordered lane-v3 axes before endpoint derivation, S2 required source-loading charges to match the actual parallel execution model, and S3 required the measured workload to implement the frozen joint anchor-eligibility and heading semantics exactly; execution governance independently reported one schema-rejection blocker.

Round-2 remediation commit 59c258534220db62e68e0cd64c59442cd2dba960 closed S1 and S2 and implemented the frozen joint three-candidate H-common, all-or-none H20/exact eligibility, heading, stationary and exact-minus-pi rules. Statistics retained one residual S3 blocker because the R04N counterpart path still exposed native 10-Hz rows to the 0.25-s exact-grid eligibility check; execution governance returned NO_BLOCKER.

Round-3 commit c6b8fb39da1af55dafae98d8b5189dc50e9e4bdd resamples counterpart positions to the tstar-anchored 0.25-s grid only inside observed support, uses immediately bracketing timestamps, permits the exact inclusive 2*dt source-gap boundary and fails closed for every strictly larger gap without extrapolation. Native-10-Hz and bit-exact inclusive/exclusive boundary fixtures close the residual.

Terminal round-3 statistics and execution-governance reviews both returned NO_BLOCKER on the exact 107-row review manifest SHA-256 69ffb92de18b057ab70d6805cc3d2043fd36b4f057683683f28b15e13e0c4956, with zero unresolved blockers or majors.
findings.summary: The W5b three-round statistics ledger closes with exact lane axes, execution-matched loading, frozen eligibility, and support-only R04N counterpart interpolation.

evidence: W5d reviewed build commit e4d9011381066882b3ef6a8bfef188705ea383cd and the exact deterministic 111-row manifest SHA-256 d0cc07da1258833a5c961e7fbe273e7428f0030da41f3366f3dfcbbe23003a59.

The Codex review lineage ended during the engine outage: execution governance had returned NO_BLOCKER, while the Codex statistics reviewer returned BLOCKED but its reasons were lost. Per the recorded PI engine-switch directive, that incomplete round is non-adjudicable and a fresh full dual Claude review supersedes it for Formal G1.

Fresh statistics reviewer claude/w5d-stat-reviewer returned NO_BLOCKER after tracing the adopted 1e-7 parity tolerance to tests/test_verifier_runtime.py:152 and observing 286 tests pass in a detached worktree with the scorer SHA verified.

The reviewer recorded one non-blocking cleanup note: dead v3 constants at scripts/hpc/prepare_research_run.py:177-182 should be fixed or deleted in the next wiring commit. Fresh execution-governance reviewer claude/w5d-exec-reviewer independently returned NO_BLOCKER, leaving zero unresolved blockers and zero unresolved majors.
findings.summary: The fresh Claude statistics review supersedes the outage-truncated Codex round and closes W5d with NO_BLOCKER; the dead-v3-constant observation is non-blocking follow-up.

evidence: Round 1 reviewed W5f commit e628d56bf5916a300aa7538ae803314709c66338: execution governance returned NO_BLOCKER, while statistics raised B1 because verification_only_for_operation incorrectly named the deserializing pilot and B2 because the FAIL-receipt promise covered pre-runtime bash aborts that cannot write a receipt.

Round 2 reviewed W5g commit 843534e61da2c8319f405d8003f2c23261cb1bc2: B1 and B2 closed through the preflight-only verification binding and the runtime-versus-pre-runtime failure split; execution governance returned NO_BLOCKER, while statistics raised B3 because digest, inherited-environment, and maintenance-lock gates could still abort without the promised Slurm-log marker.

Round 3 reviewed W5h commit 431074c561ada39d63cbfc678b11e774120a56d7: the cited top-level B3 gates gained deterministic stderr diagnostics, but both reviewers blocked on the residual silent stdlib, site-packages, and native-closure emitters.

Round 4 reviewed W5i commit 2a982607053d3ea06c5d2d55a0c1fdd688df4ecd: those three closure classes gained executed diagnostic regressions and execution governance returned NO_BLOCKER with normalized pass-path bytes unchanged; the statistics sweep retained one blocker for raw pilot-M3, duplicate preflight, and export output-root gates.

Round 5 reviewed W5j commit 7d912cd1a4a1bcd06fc27ce9dbc1683bb7a53369: the exhaustive three-operation sweep and structural no-silent-gate regression closed the residual, and both distinct reviewers returned NO_BLOCKER on the exact deterministic 111-row manifest SHA-256 d23d4c677b9d966248daec56c91f0ce187f3279703a8fdc6a8b2bae681d624e3 with zero unresolved blockers or majors.
findings.summary: The five-round W5f-W5j statistics ledger closes B1, B2, B3, and the exhaustive residual-gate sweep with terminal dual NO_BLOCKER.

evidence: Round 1 reviewed W5l commit 536fcd4e61bc6b079e0b5979dfc52eabbb927aff: its Python-side v4 validator accepted the immutable published native TSV's real-tab columns header and the full real-file audit found no other Python representation mismatch, but both reviewers returned BLOCKED because the emitted v4 shell gate still expected literal <TAB> bytes and would abort after validate-only at native:manifest:header-columns.

Round 2 reviewed W5m commit 042c638157d067dc6f409dcf46bebbda170d0568: the emitted v4-only columns-header expectation now uses real tab bytes, the published v3 literal-<TAB> contract remains unchanged, and the sweep covered every content-derived stdlib, site-packages, distribution, and native expectation against the staged immutable published files.

The real-emission regression executes all five native header guards against the exact published header bytes, and the full RQ014 suite passed 301 tests. The terminal statistics and execution-governance reviewers independently returned NO_BLOCKER on the exact deterministic 111-row manifest SHA-256 b392a42d21d70336c1b13c5cb4dde7eeca4cc19f87c8d62b7b15f2c90597bb45, leaving zero unresolved blockers and zero unresolved majors.
findings.summary: The two-round W5l-W5m statistics ledger closes the cross-layer v4 header representation defect with consistent Python and emitted-shell validation.
